features.title: Card network for Visa-branded cards
features.description: Enables authorization, clearing, and settlement of Visa credit, debit, and prepaid card transactions through participating issuers and acquirers.

features.title: Card-present and card-not-present support
features.description: Supports in-store (chip/contactless) and online/mobile (e-commerce) transaction flows, typically via an acquiring bank or PSP integration.

features.title: Tokenization and security programs
features.description: Works with industry and Visa programs such as network tokenization (where available), EMV, and fraud/security frameworks implemented by issuers, acquirers, and PSPs.

features.title: International acceptance
features.description: Facilitates cross-border card payments and currency conversion via participating banks and processors.

features.title: Dispute and chargeback frameworks
features.description: Provides network rules and processes for chargebacks, representment, and dispute management handled by acquirers/issuers and merchant PSPs.

features.title: Issuer and acquirer ecosystem
features.description: Merchants typically access Visa through acquiring banks and payment processors rather than integrating directly with Visa.

seo_description: Overview of Visa as a global card network, including typical integration options, supported payment flows, and key considerations for merchants and payment PSPs
keywords: Visa, card network, credit card, debit card, payments, card-present, card-not-present, tokenization
excerpt: Visa is a global payment card network that enables card-based transactions processed by banks and payment service providers.

availability.note: Visa-branded cards and network acceptance are available worldwide, subject to local issuer/acquirer availability and regulatory requirements.

tip: If you need to accept Visa, choose a payment service provider or acquiring bank that supports Visa and provides the gateway/API, 3DS, and compliance tooling required for your region.

faq.question: Is Visa a payment gateway or a card network?
faq.answer: Visa is a card network. Most merchants accept Visa by using a payment gateway/PSP or acquiring bank that connects to the Visa network.

faq.question: How do merchants integrate Visa payments?
faq.answer: Merchants typically integrate with a PSP or acquiring bank via their APIs, hosted checkout, or terminals. The PSP/acquirer handles connectivity to Visa and other card networks.

faq.question: Does Visa provide an API for merchants?
faq.answer: Visa offers developer products and APIs, but common card acceptance for merchants is usually implemented through a PSP/acquirer rather than direct Visa integration.

faq.question: Where is Visa available?
faq.answer: Visa has global availability, but specific services and acceptance depend on local issuers, acquirers, processors, and regulatory requirements.

faq.question: What compliance requirements apply to accepting Visa cards?
faq.answer: Merchants typically must follow PCI DSS and card-network rules. Your PSP/acquirer can help determine requirements such as 3D Secure support, fraud controls, and data handling.
